basic_info.incident_name: Formosa Plastics Propylene Fire and Explosions
basic_info.incident_name_cn: 台塑丙烯泄漏火灾爆炸事故

process_context.operating_conditions: liquid phase, pressurized hydrocarbon service at ambient temperature

failure_mechanism.root_cause: A forklift-towed trailer physically snagged and dislodged a small drain valve from a strainer in the liquid propylene system.
failure_mechanism.causal_chain: Forklift operation near piping → mechanical impact on drain valve → valve detachment → uncontrolled propylene release → rapid vaporization → flammable vapor cloud formation → ignition → explosion and sustained fire
failure_mechanism.physical_principles: Phase change (liquid to vapor), flammability limits, deflagration dynamics, vapor dispersion

protection_system.safeguard_failures: Operators could not reach manual valves due to advancing vapor cloud; flare system could not handle instantaneous large release; no physical barrier or procedural control preventing vehicle access near critical piping.
csb_recommendations: Implement rigorous management of change (MOC) for all non-routine activities near process equipment
Install permanent physical barriers to prevent vehicle contact with piping and valves
Conduct updated, site-specific HAZOP studies incorporating human factors and mechanical integrity hazards

embedding_text: Liquid propylene release occurred when a forklift-towed trailer dislodged a drain valve on a strainer in a pressurized piping system. The 'No flow' HAZOP deviation (guide word: No, parameter: flow) led to catastrophic uncontrolled release, rapid vaporization, formation of a large flammable cloud, and subsequent ignition causing an unconfined deflagration. Root cause was inadequate mechanical integrity protection and lack of physical safeguards against vehicular impact. Consequences included 16 injuries, 5-day fire, unit shutdown, and community shelter-in-place.
